originalTitle: US announces charges against 15 people in Minnesota over alleged $90m healthcare scheme
summary: Assistant US attorney general claims services in state subject to ‘shocking’ levels of fraud, which he labels a ‘crisis’
content: The Trump administration broke new ground in its offensive against alleged fraud in Minnesota’s social services on Thursday as the US Department of Justice announced charges against 15 people accused of cheating a government healthcare program to the tune of $90m. At a news conference in Minneapolis that underscored Donald Trump’s fierce focus on the state and Tim Walz, its Democratic governor, Colin McDonald, assistant US attorney general, told journalists that services had been subject to “shocking” levels of fraud, which he labelled a “crisis”. Instances included an autism program where costs to taxpayers had ballooned from just $600,000 to more than $400m in six years, he claimed. Another scheme meant to help unhoused people find and maintain housing had risen 50-fold in cost, from $2.5m annually in 2020 to more than $104m in 2024, leading to its closure the following year – meaning that services no longer exist for a vulnerable segment of the population, McDonald said. Flanked by the health secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr, and Mehmet Oz, head of the Medicare and Medicaid government health insurance programs, McDonald declared: “It is a crisis in Minnesota.” The charges brought, he said, “include the highest loss amount ever charged in a Medicaid case in Minnesota, and the largest autism fraud scheme ever charged by the Department of Justice”. “The common theme throughout these cases is fraudsters exploiting vulnerable programs and vulnerable people to enrich themselves, no matter the consequences, to the programs or to the people,” McDonald said. Thursday’s announcement followed months of complaints from Trump about alleged rampant mass fraud in Minnesota, much of it tinged with racist vitriol against the state’s Somali community. The fraud accusations were initially partly driven by a video from a rightwing social media influencer, Nick Shirley, focused on a children’s daycare centre, which had its name misspelled, and which received large sums in state and federal assistance. The administration cited fraud as a major factor behind the decision to deploy Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents into the state last winter. The decision led to mass protests on the streets of Minneapolis, and the shooting deaths of two US citizens, Renee Good and Alex Pretti, at the hands of federal agents. McDonald said the justice department had also deployed 11 “strike force prosecutors” from across the country into Minnesota, resulting in Thursday’s prosecutions. JD Vance has since been put in charge of a White House initiative to root out fraud nationwide. At the news conference, Kennedy called the prosecutions “the largest autism fraud bust in American history”, and said charges had been brought in record time. More would be seen in the near future, he added. “I want to be clear: this is an extraordinary bust today, because these kind of operations usually take many years, sometimes a decade,” said Kennedy. “This was executed with a precision and speed that is unprecedented in the history of law enforcement. “But under Vice-President Vance’s leadership and President Trump’s direction, we are going to normalize this speed.”
chineseTitle: 美国宣布对明尼苏达州 15 人提出指控，指控其涉嫌 9000 万美元的医疗保健计划
chineseSummary: 美国助理总检察长声称该州的服务欺诈程度“令人震惊”，他将其称为“危机”
chineseContent: 周四，特朗普政府在打击明尼苏达州社会服务欺诈行为方面取得了新进展，美国司法部宣布对 15 名被指控在政府医疗保健计划中作弊、金额达 9000 万美元的人提出指控。在明尼阿波利斯举行的新闻发布会上，强调了唐纳德·特朗普对该州及其民主党州长蒂姆·沃尔兹的强烈关注，美国助理司法部长科林·麦克唐纳告诉记者，服务业遭受的欺诈程度“令人震惊”，他将其称为“危机”。他声称，其中一个例子包括一个自闭症项目，该项目给纳税人带来的费用在六年内从仅仅 60 万美元飙升至超过 4 亿美元。麦克唐纳表示，另一项旨在帮助无家可归者寻找和维护住房的计划成本上涨了 50 倍，从 2020 年的每年 250 万美元增至 2024 年的超过 1.04 亿美元，导致该计划于次年关闭，这意味着针对弱势群体的服务不再存在。在卫生部长小罗伯特·F·肯尼迪和医疗保险和医疗补助政府健康保险计划负责人穆罕默德·奥兹的陪同下，麦克唐纳宣布：“这是明尼苏达州的一场危机。”他说，所提出的指控“包括明尼苏达州医疗补助案件中有史以来最高的损失金额，以及司法部有史以来指控的最大的自闭症欺诈计划”。麦克唐纳说：“这些案件的共同主题是欺诈者利用脆弱的项目和弱势人群来充实自己，无论后果如何，对项目或人民来说都是如此。”在周四宣布这一消息之前，特朗普对明尼苏达州涉嫌猖獗的大规模欺诈行为进行了数月的投诉，其中大部分都带有针对该州索马里社区的种族主义尖酸刻薄的色彩。欺诈指控最初是
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
